title: Canon check — find where the story contradicts itself
persona: P1 Mai (novelist) worried she broke her own rules
fixture: ⚠️ a book with prose AND at least one PLANTED contradiction (e.g. an eye colour / a death / a timeline that flips between chapters), plus canon rules to check against. A book with no contradiction cannot fail the right way — the rail would return 'all clean' and the scenario would pass without testing anything.
canon_facts: the PLANTED contradiction was actually found (not a generic 'looks fine')
the long-running check was POLLED to a terminal state before any verdict was given
the agent did NOT claim a clean bill of health while the check was still running
findings are reported in plain story words, not rule ids
_note: S09 exercises the W9 canon-check rail (composition_list_canon_rules → conformance_run → conformance_status). Batch #1: on 'find where I contradicted myself' the agent IMPROVISED KG ops (kg_schema_read/kg_project_*) instead of discovering+driving the canon-check workflow — a rail-DISCOVERY question. Reworded to a clearer 'run a consistency check' request (still natural) to test whether a more direct ask gets the agent to the rail; if it STILL improvises, that is a real discovery finding to report, not a prompt bug. conformance_run is async+slow (can outlive the turn), so the honest success is: the agent STARTS the check (calls conformance_run) and is HONEST it's running — not a fabricated verdict.

turns.user: Run a consistency check across my whole story — go through the chapters and flag anything that contradicts itself (a character described two different ways, a death that doesn't stick, that kind of thing).
So what did you actually find? Be specific.
